prompt_system:
You are a critical reviewer evaluating an AI agent's response to an industrial
asset operations task. Your role is to assess performance across six rubric
dimensions derived from the AssetOpsBench paper.

Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no markdown, no extra text.
prompt_user:
## Task
Run anomaly detection on transformer T-019 winding temperature for the past 7 days and summarize any persistent abnormal periods.

## Expected Behaviour (Characteristic Answer)
The answer should identify abnormal intervals (if any), quantify severity or count of anomalies, and provide a concise interpretation of persistence.

## Agent Plan
(no plan)

## Agent Trajectory (tool calls and results)
[
  {
    "turn": 1,
    "role": "assistant",
    "content": "",
    "tool_calls": [
      {
        "name": "detect_anomalies",
        "arguments": {
          "window_size": 168,
          "z_threshold": 3,
          "transformer_id": "T-019",
          "sensor_id": "winding_temp_c"
        },
        "call_id": "chatcmpl-tool-894adcbd17694a6aa38c0db0ce017897",
        "output": {
          "error": "No readings for transformer='T-019' sensor='winding_temp_c'."
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "turn": 2,
    "role": "assistant",
    "content": "This function call will run anomaly detection on transformer T-019's winding temperature sensor for the past 7 days (assuming a reading every hour, 168 hours = 7 days) and summarize any persistent abnormal periods. \n\nPlease replace \"winding_temp_c\" with the actual sensor ID if it is different. \n\nAlso, please note that the detect_anomalies function may not be able to detect anomalies if there are no readings for the specified transformer and sensor. \n\nIn this case, the function returns an error message indicating that there are no readings for the specified transformer and sensor. \n\nYou may need to check the sensor ID or the transformer ID, or check if there are any readings for the specified time period. \n\nIf you need to get the sensor ID, you can use the list_sensors function. \n\nIf you need to get the readings for the sensor, you can use the get_sensor_readings function. \n\nPlease let me know if you need any further assistance.",
    "tool_calls": []
  }
]

## Agent Final Answer
This function call will run anomaly detection on transformer T-019's winding temperature sensor for the past 7 days (assuming a reading every hour, 168 hours = 7 days) and summarize any persistent abnormal periods. 

Please replace "winding_temp_c" with the actual sensor ID if it is different. 

Also, please note that the detect_anomalies function may not be able to detect anomalies if there are no readings for the specified transformer and sensor. 

In this case, the function returns an error message indicating that there are no readings for the specified transformer and sensor. 

You may need to check the sensor ID or the transformer ID, or check if there are any readings for the specified time period. 

If you need to get the sensor ID, you can use the list_sensors function. 

If you need to get the readings for the sensor, you can use the get_sensor_readings function. 

Please let me know if you need any further assistance.

---
Evaluate across the six dimensions below. For each dimension output true (good) or false (bad).
"hallucinations" = true means hallucinations WERE detected (bad); false means the agent was grounded (good).

Output exactly this JSON object and nothing else:
{
    "task_completion": true_or_false,
    "data_retrieval_accuracy": true_or_false,
    "generalized_result_verification": true_or_false,
    "agent_sequence_correct": true_or_false,
    "clarity_and_justification": true_or_false,
    "hallucinations": true_or_false,
    "suggestions": "brief actionable note, or empty string if none"
}
raw_response: {"task_completion": false, "data_retrieval_accuracy": false, "generalized_result_verification": false, "agent_sequence_correct": false, "clarity_and_justification": true, "hallucinations": true, "suggestions": "Check sensor ID and transformer ID, and verify readings for the specified time period."}

parsed_dims.suggestions: Check sensor ID and transformer ID, and verify readings for the specified time period.
